tasks: Built reusable, component-based features in Cascade CMS using JavaScript, HTML, CSS, Apache Velocity, and Tailwind CSS to support scalable, system-wide website development across campuses.
Met with schools and units to gather feature requests during stakeholder meetings, translating requirements into new CMS components and enhancements.
Collaborated with Mānoa Marketing and Branding to review proposed features and component changes, ensuring alignment with brand standards and consistent adoption across schools and departments.
Developed and maintained shared CMS templates and components (news listings, directories, navigation, cards, RSS feeds, factoids, headers, footers, etc.) used across multiple schools and units.
Supported large-scale website migrations, including Admissions and Law School sites, ensuring accurate content transfer, structural integrity, and minimal disruption to stakeholders.

Implemented WCAG 2.1 accessibility standards and system-wide language access solutions, including Hawaiian diacritics support across templates, navigation, headers, footers, and page-level components.
Authored internal documentation for Tailwind CSS usage, custom CMS components, and bug fixes to support team knowledge sharing and long-term maintainability.
Point Cloud Processing: Perform QC and multi-station adjustments to align and optimize scan data for accuracy, ensuring consistency with control scans.
Data Registration: Merging and registering multiple scan stations to produce a unified, high-accuracy point cloud.
Linework Preparation: Identifying and labeling all stationary features (e.g., buildings, curbs, signs, utility infrastructure) to prepare data for CAD workflows.
Topographic Mapping: Creating accurate topographic maps by interpreting linework and integrating scan data to support design, planning, and engineering workflows.
